title: NSW experimental benchmark: the +$1,794 the observational pipeline must recover
gold_source: Dehejia & Wahba (1999, JASA 94(448)) NSW male-sample experimental benchmark: treatment-control difference in 1978 earnings of about +$1,794. This exact constant is pinned, with the dataset, in the parent AERS repo's audited numeric benchmark (benchmark/tasks/lalonde-recovery.toml, 'experimental_att = 1794.0'; data at benchmark/data/, from the public Dehejia-Wahba NSW files), where a deterministic checker recomputes it from the shipped data - so the gold is a measured, recomputable fact rather than a transcription from memory.
description: The canonical stress test for observational causal inference. The NSW job-training program was a randomized experiment, so the experimental contrast (~+$1,794 in 1978 earnings for the male sample) is ground truth. LaLonde (1986) showed that replacing the experimental controls with survey controls (PSID/CPS) and running naive regressions flips the estimate negative; Dehejia & Wahba (1999) showed propensity-score methods on the right subsample recover the experimental benchmark. A Stage 3 pipeline pointed at the NSW-plus-survey-controls data must (1) surface the covariate imbalance rather than hide it, and (2) produce an adjusted estimate that is positive and near the experimental benchmark - matching the parent repo's lalonde-recovery golds.

primary_coefficients.name: adjusted_att
primary_coefficients.note: Adjusted (matched / weighted / doubly-robust) ATT on 1978 earnings. Observational methods are not guaranteed to nail the experimental number, hence the looser rel_tol than a same-data replication; the sign and rough magnitude are the load-bearing facts.

candidate_contract.note: The naive unadjusted contrast on the composite (non-experimental) sample is strongly negative; reporting that number here means the pipeline skipped the balancing step.
